input_dict.question: What was the record for the head football coach of this private four-year Christian-based college founded in Salina, Kansas, United States?
input_dict.current_output: Kansas Wesleyan University's head football coaches with recorded win–loss totals in the provided passages include Coach Miller (0–1), W. F. Ragle (5–4), C. L. Williams (9–5), and Ron Dupree (11–18). Matt Drinkall was hired as Kansas Wesleyan's head coach in January 2014 (his win–loss record is not given in the supplied passages).

Header: Kansas Wesleyan University — head football coach records (cited passages)

- College identity: "Kansas Wesleyan University" is a "private four-year Christian-based college" "founded in Salina, Kansas, United States in 1886."

  - "W. F. Ragle" — "fifth head football coach" at Kansas Wesleyan; "serving for one season, in 1915" and "compiling a record of 5–4."
  - "Matt Drinkall" — "hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas" in "January 2014" (no record given).
- Ambiguity / missing facts:
  - Ambiguous target: the phrase "the head football coach" could refer to multiple persons (Coach Miller, W. F. Ragle, Matt Drinkall). Label: multiple candidate coaches.
  - Missing fact: the win–loss record for the current head coach (Matt Drinkall) is not provided in the passages. Bridge entity: "Matt Drinkall" / "Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes football".
- Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
  1. Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes football — inspect lede/infobox and "Head coaches" or "Coaching history" sections (to get official head coach list and records).
  2. Matt Drinkall — fetch full page, check "Career" or "Head coaching record" section for his win–loss record.

hop2_docs: Matt Drinkall | Matt Drinkall is an American football coach.  In January 2014 he was hired as the head coach of the Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes in Salina, Kansas.  He has worked previously as a football coach at the high school and college level.

Ron Dupree | Ron Dupree is a former American football coach.  Dupree was the 15th head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for three seasons, from 1979 to 1980 and again in 1996.  His coaching record at Kansas Wesleyan was 11–18.

Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes | The Kansas Wesleyan Coyotes are the athletic teams that represent Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas.  They are part of the National Association of Intercollegiate Athletics (NAIA), competing in the Kansas Collegiate Athletic Conference (KCAC).

W. F. Ragle | W. F. Ragle was an American football coach.  He was the fifth head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1915 and compiling a record of 5–4.

Zora G. Clevenger | Zora G. Clevenger (December 12, 1881 – November 24, 1970) was an American football, basketball, and baseball player, coach, and pioneering athletic director.  He served as the head football coach at Nebraska Wesleyan University (1908–1910), the University of Tennessee (1911–1915), and Kansas State University (1916–1919), compiling a record of 47–32–7.  Clevenger was also the head basketball coach at Indiana University (1904–1906), Nebraska Wesleyan (1907–1911), Tennessee (1911–1916), and Kansas State (1916–1919), and was baseball coach at Indiana (1905–1906), Nebraska Wesleyan (1908–1911), Tennessee (1911–1916), and Kansas State (1919–1921).  Clevenger served as the athletic director at Kansas State (1916–1920), the University of Missouri (1921–1923), and Indiana (1923–1946).  He was inducted into the College Football Hall of Fame as a player in 1968.

C. L. Williams | C. L. Williams was an American football coach.  He was the third head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for two seasons, from 1908 to 1909, compiling a record of 9–5.

Coach Miller | Coach Miller was an American football coach.  He was the second head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for one season, in 1906, and compiling a record of 0–1.

visible_gold_support.title: C. L. Williams
visible_gold_support.sentence: He was the third head football coach at Kansas Wesleyan University in Salina, Kansas, serving for two seasons, from 1908 to 1909, compiling a record of 9–5.
